model.idea: A static file that RUNS. The repo ships the logic (an ES module — or WASM bytecode) as a content-addressed, pinnable frame. A caller loads it into a sandboxed, offscreen (headless) iframe and calls it over a private MessagePort to get real/live computed data. No server: the compute runs on the caller's CPU.
model.verify_before_exec: The caller recomputes SHA-256 of the bytes and checks it against the pinned sha8 BEFORE executing — the same Layer-2 verify-before-exec an MCP host does, except the frame is code.
model.isolation: iframe sandbox='allow-scripts' (no allow-same-origin) => opaque origin, no access to the host DOM/cookies/storage; talks only over a private channel.
model.live_data: The cell may fetch() any CORS-open source from inside the sandbox (e.g. the already-published static Dataverse API) and compute over it — live, still with no server you run.
model.wasm: Swap a .mjs frame for a .wasm frame and the build is identical (it content-addresses bytes); only the loader changes (WebAssembly.instantiate instead of import). That is the literal-'bytecode' variant.

cells.name: twin
cells.description: Pure compute over caller-supplied rows, plus live() which fetches a CORS-open static API from inside the sandbox and computes over it.
